title: Draag
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Species |image=[[File:Draag-1.jpg]] |name=Draag |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]]{{CSWECite|I|203|Draag}} |class=[[Reptile/Legends|Reptilian]] |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=Pale [[Color/Legends|yellow]]{{GamerCite|10|State of the Arts}} |haircolor= |feathers= |eyecolor= |distinctions=Four nostrils, horns |lifespan= |origin=[[Ninzam VI]] |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|Wait here. Keep your eyes open.|A Draag supervisor gives orders to a Guineo underling|}} '''Draags''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of stocky, [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ninzam VI]]. Due to their cold-[[Blood/Legends|blooded]] nature, they often wore temperature-regulating [[Environment suit/Legends|suits]] while away from their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. The species's aggression, intelligence, and pushiness made them natural supervisors, and some Draags also used their adeptness with [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] to work as security forces. In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], several Draags worked for [[Haugg Nerf Industries]] in the [[Industrial District/Legends|Industrial District]] of the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Among them were two Draag fugitives, [[Regg Kuuga]] and [[Slaag Lado]]. Both Draags were captured by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] as he made his way through the plant to capture its boss, [[Groff Haugg]]. ==Biology and appearance== {{Quote|It's cold in here!''"
"''Yeah, you got that right. You get used to it though.|A Guineo worker and Draag supervisor|}} Draags were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]], each of whom was covered in pale, [[Color/Legends|yellow]] skin and had a stout, bulky frame. Each Draag had a wide, rounded [[Skeleton/Legends|cranium]] that sat atop a burly neck. The face consisted of a wide, lipless mouth, a small puggish [[Nose/Legends|nose]] with four nostrils, and a pair of squinty [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] under heavy brows. A small [[Ear/Legends|ear]] hole perforated each side of the head, and several bony protrusions grew from the top and back of the skull. Draags had four-fingered [[Limb/Legends|hands]]. The species was cold-[[Blood/Legends|blooded]] and divided into both male and female [[Gender/Legends|sexes]].[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Bounty Hunter'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] ==Society and culture== Draags had a reputation for ferocity and intelligence, and they were known for their inherent knack at using [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] weapons. They had a tendency to boss others around, a trait that made them naturals at supervisory roles, especially on security details. The species had access to [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]]-standard technology, and some Draags became experts in demolitions. The species employed specialized [[Environment suit/Legends|environment suits]] to regulate their body temperatures in extreme climates; a typical suit consisted of a full-body jumpsuit, a pair of [[Glove/Legends|gloves]] and [[Boot/Legends|boots]], a chestplate, and a helmet. A strap stretched across the upper lip just below the nostrils. ==History== The [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Ninzam VI]] gave rise to the Draag species, and after their introduction to [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] beyond—an event that occurred at some point between [[3000 BBY|3000]] and [[1004 BBY]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]''—the [[Ninzam system/Legends|Ninzam system]] became integrated into the [[Ryndellian sector/Legends|Ryndellian sector]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} From 1004 to [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], the Draag [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] lay within unaligned or contested [[Realspace/Legends|space]] as the [[New Sith Wars]] raged. By the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], it fell within [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]-controlled space. The Draag's home region had an average of 10 to 100 million inhabitants per [[Star system/Legends|star system]] c. [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]]. By [[137 ABY]], the species's territory was within the area claimed by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Krayt]] as part of his [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Galactic Empire]]. ==Draags in the galaxy== {{Quote|Why we gotta stand guard in here? The boss afraid somebody's going to steal a bunch of frozen nerfs?''"
"''It ain't the nerfs he's got us guarding, you idiot!''"
"''You don't have to get mean. I'm new. They don't tell me much.|A Guineo worker and Draag supervisor|}} [[File:Draag_orders_Guineo.jpg|thumb|left|Draags often worked as supervisors over members of other species, such as Guineo.]] Draags who left Ninzam VI were often hired to work in supervisory roles at large factories; they were especially valued as heads of security details. Leaving their homeworld often subjected Draags to cool temperatures with which their natural metabolisms could not cope, so off-world members of the species often relied on temperature-controlling body suits. In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], a [[Nerf/Legends|nerf]]-meat-processing plant known as [[Haugg Nerf Industries]], located within the [[Industrial District/Legends|Industrial District]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], employed several Draags, all of whom wore orange jumpsuits to contend with the cool temperatures inside the factory and carried blasters. [[Unidentified Draag supervisor|One such Draag]] worked in the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] where the plant received shipments from incoming [[Speeder/Legends|speeders]]. Although other employees were kept ignorant of such dealings, including a [[Guineo]] [[Unidentified Guineo guard|guard]] under the Draag's supervision, the hangar chief knew that the plant was actually a cover for the illicit processing and transshipment of potent [[Spice/Legends|narcotics]] known as [[Death stick/Legends|death sticks]]. When a [[Pilot/Legends|pilotless]] speeder arrived one day, the Draag left to inform [[Groff Haugg]], the plant's owner. He was stopped by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], who had infiltrated the plant to claim the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on Haugg's head.''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' In his pursuit of Haugg, Fett encountered two wanted Draag criminals in the factory. The first of these, [[Regg Kuuga]], was wanted by the [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant police]] for stealing high-value [[Hospital ship/Legends|medical frigates]], stripping them for parts, and building [[Attack cruiser/Legends|attack cruisers]] to harass tourists to the planet. The Draag was attacked by the bounty hunter on an elevated walkway. {{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Bounty Hunter}} Kuuga was then captured alive. {{Endgame}} [[Slaag Lado]], another plant employee, also had a bounty on his head; with ties to the [[Calamasthon Defiance]] and known Draag demolitionists, Lado was a [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] in the view of the [[Mon Calamari Civil Authority]], who held him responsible for initiating a seismic catastrophe in the [[Salinrerian Sea]] that resulted in untold harm to the environment and a state of constant rain.{{CSWECite|II|240|Lado, Slaag}} When Fett found Lado at the end of a corridor, the bounty hunter captured him. ==Behind the scenes== The [[April]] [[2002]] [[State of the Arts|preview]] for the [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'', published in [[Star Wars Gamer 10|''Star Wars Gamer'' 10]] magazine, includes an illustration of a Draag in an enviro-suit, drawn by [[Cory Allemeier]], and represents the species's introduction to ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]]. In the second level of the video game itself, released in [[December]] 2002, the bounty hunter Jango Fett must fight through Draags and their associates in his quest to apprehend the criminal Groff Haugg. On the way, Fett may capture or [[Death/Legends|kill]] two Draags who have bounties on their heads. Such a course is not necessary to progress in the game, but ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[December]] [[2008]], establishes that Fett does in fact capture the fugitive Slaag Lado in its entry on Lado. The narrative above assumes Fett also captures Regg Kuuga, as doing so provides the bounty hunter with the maximum reward for the level. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{GamerCite|10|State of the Arts}} {{1stp}} *[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Bounty Hunter'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Draags}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |pl=Draagowie }} [[Category:Mid Rim sentient species]] [[Category:Reptilian sentient species]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Draag]] }}
